concept: Meal / Food Eaten in One Occasion
description: Food prepared or gathered for one eating occasion, and by extension the occasion itself. The company, pace, formality, and amount all vary; 'nuora' (food) names what nourishes, while 'nurome' gathers some of it into a particular sitting.
sound_symbolism: Deep 'nu' starts with the tongue at the teeth and resonance in the nose. Stressed 'ro' turns that hold into a roll and round 'o', then final 'me' closes softly at the lips. Low and full throughout, the sound comes to a quiet balanced rest.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'lo mia ha nurome nuola.' (We eat this meal.) Time words identify a morning or evening meal without requiring separate breakfast or dinner roots. Context distinguishes the eating occasion from the food offered.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A meal gives sharing a concrete object: who prepared it, who can eat it, and who has not yet been served can all remain visible.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Meals followed work, light, season, and available fuel more often than a fixed clock.

semantic_domains.community: food gathered for solitary or shared eating
